data.narrative:
Astroscale, a public-stage space & aerospace company founded by Mitsunobu Okada, has raised $400M+ and currently trades at a $900M valuation. The peer universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers across the space sector, including Rocket Lab, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace.

Given Astroscale's public status and disclosure constraints, peer-median valuation is the applicable triangulation method. Three same-sector, same-stage peers yield a median valuation of $7.0B, with a range spanning $6.3B (Firefly Aerospace) to $37.0B (Rocket Lab). The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in business model maturity and revenue scale within space & aerospace. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied; the sector median of 49.8x cannot be meaningfully deployed without reliable revenue data from the target.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $6.0B to $8.0B, centered on the peer median of $7.0B. This implies a significant spread between Astroscale's current $900M valuation and peer-derived fair value, though the $6.3B–$37.0B peer range itself underscores material uncertainty in space-sector valuations. Key risks include the sustainability of public-market multiples in this sector and whether Astroscale's specific operational metrics and contract pipeline justify positioning within this peer band.
